title: Deguelia sumatrana
intro: Deguelia sumatrana is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deguelia sumatrana include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Deguelia sumatrana is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Deguelia is part of the tribe Sophoreae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its genetic relationships with other plants. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Deguelia sumatrana.
